body:

---
| Friend, I recently took a stand against sanctuary cities here in Ohio and let me tell you why I feel so strongly about this.

**All of these are unacceptable!** Our priority is to keep our people here at home safe. I believe that if officials don't want to enforce deportation laws, they should be held accountable. If these officials are willing to stick up for illegals staying in our cities, they must take the blame for the criminal actions that come along with it—plain and simple. Again, thank you for showing your support as we work to keep Ohio safe. I will do everything in my power to **stop sanctuary cities** and **stop radical Islamic terrorism.** Best, Josh

name: The White House
subject: Your 1600 Daily
body:
Everything White House for 3/17/2017. |

**FEATURED**
The Easter Egg Roll ticket lottery closes tomorrow, Saturday, March 18th. Be sure to enter the lottery today.
[ ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e816855d5e95d25d0f86637faf75dd4f1c27ee0aef4c9a355a7149ac85254c55e83e224fb55dedef709c62e4601ef3aa63aaa4e)

**WHITE HOUSE MEMO**
President Trump is focused today on several of his top priorities: making health insurance more affordable and accessible, taking care of our great veterans, training America's incredible workforce for the manufacturing jobs of the future, and strengthening the United States' relationship with our important ally, Germany.

**GET INVOLVED**
Be sure to follow [@POTUS](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e8168554062184bdc8ec4abeea03f903833df1a74da1bee42906503f7f947c68d7dd27715243582e114b8c834408b6b23aa23ef) and [@WhiteHouse](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e816855f62a70b6169fe651634ea6e1146bc013ff67a530941ec44b9322d7d23105e5fc395acd9573f85c6c5e86046f28bcef41) on Twitter for real time updates.

**FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP**
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e8168552f5b1d00dbffa172d7714e5ca2d4179f00109eb844ebfe8f4a0057c3b79068da55325289914b4a5e4c599698136229cc)

**OVAL OFFICE HIGHLIGHTS**
President Trump wishes Mr. Ray Chavez, the oldest living Pearl Harbor veteran, a happy 105th birthday.
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e816855ecc21750e7d07e86b1713bebb527fe0cac34710edbf16b0fbbc79e2beb00858338ebcdf6ad07d0712018f193433a1cea)
[View Letter](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e816855ecc21750e7d07e86b1713bebb527fe0cac34710edbf16b0fbbc79e2beb00858338ebcdf6ad07d0712018f193433a1cea)

President Trump pens a letter to Speaker of the House Paul Ryan regarding the President's FY 2018 budget.
[Read Letter](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e8168551c9ea1ce103d17a4da408183f8fa0257699fdfc301fbf234abcac80e47d8ae2e99a9506dce6c46ec98ebf1c699dded9a)

President Trump meets with the Taoiseach of Ireland, Enda Kenny.
[Read More](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e8168555217b9596ca9d19944fd54d1e092cd01688d68e8ec139dc95f75df99fc74b8d9a123b4f30e662c53378979e5bda3c77a)

**PRESS ROOM**
Watch yesterday's press briefing with Press Secretary Sean Spicer and OMB Director Mick Mulvaney [here.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=508bb93e6e816855c3d03bc37ef59d015cc1ef691367cfb0dd88d7aa4d53b43b6870330b40043907776ad47caca111abd8d5aa34d727a62d)

**PRESIDENT TRUMP'S SCHEDULE**
* 9:30AM: President Trump meets with the Republican Study Committee
* 10:00AM: President Trump leads a listening session on veterans' affairs
* 11:30AM: President Trump has a meeting with the Chancellor of Germany
* 11:45AM: President Trump leads a bilateral meeting with the Chancellor of Germany
* 12:30PM: President Trump leads a roundtable discussion on vocational training with American and German business leaders
* 1:20PM: President Trump participates in a joint press conference with the Chancellor of Germany
* 1:50PM: President Trump hosts a working luncheon with the Chancellor of Germany

subject: It's official - she's in!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello,
I wanted to make sure you didn't miss the exciting news from yesterday. My friend, Joyce Craig, is running for Mayor of Manchester.
I'm with Joyce because she knows that we can and must do better to make the Queen City a better place for families to live, work, and raise their children.
**[I'm asking you-- if you're ready for a new Mayor with fresh vision and energy, donate $3 or more directly to Joyce's campaign. Like me, she's counting on grassroots supporters to power her campaign, so every dollar you can give helps](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd12/2a380d31/15e49698/fee95b/342819145/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jcmwh1?refcode=JCMWH1").**

name: Tim Kaine
subject: even worse than we thought
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

**It 's even worse than we thought. **

The nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office (CBO) has completed its analysis of TrumpCare, the Republicans' proposed replacement for Obamacare. They found that **the Republican bill would result in _24 million people_ losing their health insurance by 2026.**

Folks showed up to town halls across the country to tell the GOP how important their health care was to them -- and now it's clear that the GOP did not listen. **[Will you add your name alongside mine and tell Republicans that TrumpCare is unacceptable?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=84xx5T8POh3Z5ta6fMSfO0Th4%2FxcLNck)**

Here are just a few more problems I have with this bill:
* Americans would see their premiums spike -- especially seniors

* Virginia and other states would be forced to make steep cuts to Medicaid

* Health insurance companies would no longer be required to cover maternity care or birth control

* Planned Parenthood, which cares for 30,000 Virginians every year, would be completely defunded

* Finally, while taking away care from people who have trouble affording it, the bill would give the wealthiest individuals a sizeable _tax cut_.

Here's the bottom line. **President Trump said repeatedly that no one would lose coverage under the Republicans ' plan. According to the CBO, this bill shatters that promise.** I intend to speak out against this bad bill -- and I hope you'll join me.

**[Will you add your name to tell Republicans that Virginia won't stand for millions of people losing their health coverage?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=dReEeUXOlxWpfrukAAwRZ0Th4%2FxcLNck)**

Thank you,
Tim

body:

Peter,

Since we launched Run to Win, the numbers we're seeing have exceeded our wildest expectations.

Since Election Day, we've had nearly 10,000 women from _**all 50 states**_ tell us they're interested in running for state and local office. That means we have to expand our efforts to help them... a lot.

To support women running in all 50 states in the 2017 and 2018 elections, we're going to need a donor network for them in every state too.

**Right now there are 738 committed 2017 EMILY's List members in Virginia, but according to our records, you're not a 2017 member yet.**

**[Donate $3 before midnight tomorrow to become a 2017 EMILY's List member and help elect more pro-choice Democratic women at every level.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d73/24c69fd9/f3662e40/18e013c3/3184300753/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170313_email_MarchMem1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+before+midnight+tomorrow+to+if+&utm_campaign=WQEFR318N_digital_email_20170317_MicroGoal_n-all&source=WQEFR318N_digital_email_20170317_MicroGoal_n-all")**

When you donate, we'll put your membership card in the mail right away.

EMILY's List women from coast to coast have been fighting Donald Trump and his fellow Republicans' disastrous policies. But it's going to take major backup from the women we're recruiting right now to fully stop the GOP's radical agenda.

This is our very last Membership Week of the year, and it's over tomorrow at midnight. We're so close to hitting our state-by-state goals, which will make sure we can keep up the fight at the local level too.

**Donate $3 now to become a 2017 EMILY's List member and support our women running for office in Virginia:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/March-Membership-Week](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d73/24c69fd9/f3662e40/18e015c4/3184300753/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170313_email_MarchMem1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgMarchMembershipW&utm_campaign=WQEFR318N_digital_email_20170317_MicroGoal_n-all&source=WQEFR318N_digital_email_20170317_MicroGoal_n-all")**

Leigh K. Warren

name: The White House
subject: LAST CHANCE: 139th White House Easter Egg Roll Lottery
body:
Tomorrow at 12PM EST is your LAST CHANCE to enter. |
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4260509f6b561851a4e84a584109b165b983299e09309ee7c48859695321064572d872779cb82885d5e4e46a4a964bb078fb7083fdf5beb5)

The White House Easter Egg Roll is a timeless White House tradition, dating back to 1878 and the presidency of President Rutherford B. Hayes. President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump are honored to continue the traditions of the past, while weaving new traditions into the fabric of our Nation’s history.

The 139th White House Easter Egg Roll will be held on April 17, 2017, on the South Lawn of the White House. It will be a fun-filled day for the whole family, filled with live entertainment, and, of course, the traditional rolling of the Easter eggs!

**Tomorrow, March 18th, is the LAST DAY to enter the lottery for your chance to attend.[Visit recreation.gov now to enter the lottery.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4260509f6b561851ca40246a109ac82487963bd489d54360a2d61e8c5e479d7fa4a3e039428c4d5cb139b101c45dd04e43e60c539ec218fa)**[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4260509f6b561851a4e84a584109b165b983299e09309ee7c48859695321064572d872779cb82885d5e4e46a4a964bb078fb7083fdf5beb5)
Entering the lottery is free of charge, and winners will be notified via email on Friday, March 31, 2017. But you must enter now before the **_lottery closes tomorrow at 12PM EST!_**

**Don’t miss this time-honored tradition and once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.[Enter the lottery now for your chance to attend the 139th White House Easter Egg Roll.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4260509f6b561851ca40246a109ac82487963bd489d54360a2d61e8c5e479d7fa4a3e039428c4d5cb139b101c45dd04e43e60c539ec218fa)**

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4260509f6b561851347b4da576ba96555ae805d45379f3e23bdf9885bf63eec0c25fadf696515d4ee29485c9761db9e1fdd77b1447c00014) [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4260509f6b561851cad4454b3cbf7f2f4d8b6ddb4e2db5600c6682a53f73d16e8b8cbf0f58032c8ac6ad2259bbf905573526a878428105b6)[Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4f71033b72e02de74365f940e2f202946f613c16b2a3e203b950a81299900907714e29ed2211682720a369a0d58fe1cf5)

**_March Madness_**
Please forgive this intrusion in the middle of March Madness, but we've got a little "March Madness" of our own with all the news and events going on! Last Friday, I traveled to [Dearborn County with Governor Holcomb](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9e98063c8c&e=1acd9bb3d6) for their annual Lincoln Day Dinner while Pete Seat [visited Gibson County](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4bb74ad967&e=1acd9bb3d6) for theirs. Both [Seema Verma](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=32b16f7304&e=1acd9bb3d6) and former[ Sen. Dan Coats](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1001bd07a0&e=1acd9bb3d6) were confirmed this week as CMS Administrator and Director of National Intelligence, respectively; the U.S. House of Representatives [continues to hold hearings](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=945b8b1638&e=1acd9bb3d6) on the American Health Care Act; President Trump [unveiled his budget](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a4deb7f8b1&e=1acd9bb3d6) proposal; and our Indiana General Assembly is in the thick of addressing major issues before Sine Die comes next month. The fun - and the work - never stops! Now, back to basketball.

\- Kyle
(Photo: Governor Holcomb, Chairman Hupfer and Dearborn County Chairman Jim Thatcher catch up before the Dearborn County Lincoln Day Dinner on Friday, March 10)

name: Tom Perez
subject: ADD YOUR NAME: Stand with Democrats to defeat Donald's budget
body:
We need to oppose Donald Trump's budget.
---
**Add your name to oppose Trump's disastrous budget proposal:

** **[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Trump-Budget1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170317_tp_nd&source=em_20170317_tp_nd")**
Friend --

Budgets are moral documents -- they reflect the values of a leader. Donald Trump released the outline for his first budget as president yesterday. There's only one way to look at it: it's a direct assault on working Americans.

**[Democrats are going to fight this budget tooth-and-nail. If you're with us, add your name right now.](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Trump-Budget1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Democrats+are+going+to+fight+this+budget&utm_campaign=em_20170317_tp_nd&source=em_20170317_tp_nd")**

There's very little that isn't on the chopping block. Job training programs, public education, public health -- all slated to be slashed or killed altogether.

Dozens of federal agencies and programs covering everything from low-income heating assistance to worker safety to public broadcasting will be gone.

This budget is bad for cities, which will see big cuts to important affordable housing and job creation programs. It's bad for rural communities that helped put Trump in office, where he is reducing or eliminating major employment and infrastructure investments.

And it's calamitous for our planet. Trump's 31 percent cut to the EPA will eliminate 50 programs, including President Obama's plan to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and the EPA's climate change research.

You might be asking, with all those cuts, where is the money going? Trump has given us his answer -- a massive buildup in military spending, more funding for Trump's deportation force to terrorize immigrant communities and separate families, and, yes, $4 billion dollars for that damn border wall.

My friend Joe Biden has a saying: "Don't tell me what you value. Show me your budget, and I'll tell you what you value."

This budget paints a pretty clear picture of what -- and whom -- Donald Trump values. And why we desperately need to fight back.

**Join us now. Say you'll stand with Democrats to defeat Donald Trump's budget:**

**[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Trump-Budget1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170317_tp_nd&source=em_20170317_tp_nd")**
Thanks,

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This budget paints a pretty clear picture of what -- and whom -- Donald Trump values. And why we desperately need to fight back. Join us now. **_[SB244-FN-A](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbT0pW52ma3LTMrghVm-2FI6cWOrzttPYXIL8ZgL2Ta1Vr7lwn9kLeyG-2Bu4VusIGGPWcUwWEReyCmtrOGPWs8lTyHSwXg90ULNornHC5ArUtZqc9DKE0nP6l4gtdR55HZoD7RLz38ohoJM7TW99wuO076zOaek0ZxEW4jfiB2HDJZ8Z_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Mc76PLKGX5hkjxUSafztcy4zc2tIaU1iD8bcINoEFSOdxiParkG5NjWB1BxsudWdY3-2FImDTWfGIycVItuI0nai-2FRdvILFjhbQokppH4yKR6GBhICF0ryMmP2pa3V5zWH0Sn6zmsO5q2ZZgKDLZtoNESe6gMbe9jHzcJ-2B9wMkCZfnuXK1VX8UyrzA9RLyKEG58FSQQyaKjcAsqCRmEpO4qUBoPtSygdbVhMlJYdK6RMThd3XDgdZfzOCZY59vMVvtGdKjVvn3Fg13U1UlVhrqvHg-3D-3D)_** Relative to exemption of income from taxation under the tax on interest and dividends.
Republicans continue to lead the way, fighting to create a business-friendly environment that helps create good-paying jobs and stands up for our hard-working citizens. With Republican Governor Sununu in the corner office, no longer will the Democrats be a stumbling block to our economic prosperity.
Also this week, Supreme Court Justice Nominee Judge Neil Gorsuch is receiving across the board praise as being principled, qualified, and a good fit for the current court. We look forward to Sens. Shaheen and Hassan putting aside their partisan politics and supporting this nomination. The Senate hearing begins March 20th.
We need your help with letters to the editor and social media posts to help spread the message that Republicans are actively making NH more business friendly. Please reply to this email today to let us know if you would be interested in authoring a letter, an op ed, or helping us with social media. We look forward to hearing from you!
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

| **Innis applauds Senate for preventing job killing legislation**
CONCORD, NH – Today, the New Hampshire Senate rejected another job killing minimum wage increase. Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle), Chair of the Commerce Committee, issued the following statement.
“Raising the minimum wage to $12 per hour would come at the expense of entry level jobs,” said Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle). “An overwhelming majority of minimum wage employees are teenagers or entry-level workers seeking the skills and experience that will help them gain higher paying jobs in the future.”
“At a time when New Hampshire has the lowest unemployment in the nation at 2.6 percent, businesses are organically paying well above the minimum wage in order to attract and retain good workers. Raising the minimum wage at this time is unnecessary, irresponsible and would have negative unintended consequences at the expense of Granite State youth

clean_body: | | | NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, MARCH 17th | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Friends, This week the Republicans in the New Hampshire Senate have taken another step in the right direction to ensure protections for small businesses by voting to advance the following legislation: ** Authorizing individuals and businesses to purchase health insurance from out-of-state companies. Reducing the rates of the business profits tax and the business enterprise tax. Establishing the small business jobs fund and tax credit. Relative to exemption of income from taxation under the tax on interest and dividends. Republicans continue to lead the way, fighting to create a business-friendly environment that helps create good-paying jobs and stands up for our hard-working citizens. With Republican Governor Sununu in the corner office, no longer will the Democrats be a stumbling block to our economic prosperity. Also this week, Supreme Court Justice Nominee Judge Neil Gorsuch is receiving across the board praise as being principled, qualified, and a good fit for the current court. We look forward to Sens. Shaheen and Hassan putting aside their partisan politics and supporting this nomination. The Senate hearing begins March 20th. We need your help with letters to the editor and social media posts to help spread the message that Republicans are actively making NH more business friendly. Please reply to this email today to let us know if you would be interested in authoring a letter, an op ed, or helping us with social media. We look forward to hearing from you! Sincerely, Jeanie | | | Join the Grassroots Circle! | | The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. | | | | Intern with the NH GOP! | | Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. | | | Upcoming Events | ---|---|--- | Stratford County GOP Monthly Meeting March 18 - 9:00 AM Pink Cadillac Diner, 17 Farmington Rd, Rochester, NH Fundraising Reception for Senator Ruth Ward March 28 - 5:00P PM Capital Club, 11 South Main St, Concord, NH 03301 Nashua GOP Roast of Jennifer Horn March 30 - 6:30 PM Marriott Event Center, 2200 Southward Drive, Nashua, NH For more information NH Young Republican Annual Meeting April 1 - 1:00 PM Shaskeen Pub, 909 Elm St, Manchester, NH For more information | | | | | | Photo of the Week | ---|---|--- Governor Sununu & members of the Pelham VFW at the Pease Air Force Base welcome home ceremony on Sunday | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | This week's spotlight shines on the . Chaired by John Spottiswood, the Pelham GOP held numerous community outreach events, knocked doors for all Republican candidates and participated in dozens of phone banks in 2016. Recently, the Hillsborough County GOP awarded them with the following: Best Event, Best Social Media, Best Voter Registration Drive, and Most Improved Town committee (for the second year in a row!) Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | Innis applauds Senate for preventing job killing legislation CONCORD, NH – Today, the New Hampshire Senate rejected another job killing minimum wage increase. Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle), Chair of the Commerce Committee, issued the following statement. “Raising the minimum wage to $12 per hour would come at the expense of entry level jobs,” said Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle). “An overwhelming majority of minimum wage employees are teenagers or entry-level workers seeking the skills and experience that will help them gain higher paying jobs in the future.” “At a time when New Hampshire has the lowest unemployment in the nation at 2.6 percent, businesses are organically paying well above the minimum wage in order to attract and retain good workers. Electing Jon Ossoff to Washington Republican Tom Price's seat in Georgia's sixth district is our best chance to add a Democrat to our firewall against any dangerous policy Donald Trump puts forth.
With access to quality, affordable health care on the line, the stakes really couldn't be higher. Will you help out?
